page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system features 170.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.08 GB per core. Acceleration is delivered via two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 80 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate a significant performance divergence between CPU-bound tasks and GPU-accelerated workloads. Single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations score in the average range. Conversely, the server achieves top-tier status in the top 10% for large language model inference, demonstrating high throughput for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models.

From a resource density perspective, the cost profile of this instance is heavily weighted toward its GPU capabilities. While the system memory and multi-core CPU capabilities are balanced, the weak single-core CPU performance represents a clear tradeoff. Utilizing this server for non-GPU workloads results in low cost efficiency relative to the hardware class.

This server is designed for workloads that can offload computation to the dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs. Appropriate applications include large language model inference, deep learning training, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks. It is less suitable for single-threaded CPU tasks, general-purpose web serving, or high-capacity local storage operations.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 12 physical Intel Xeon cores, and 170.0 GB of system memory. It is equipped with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 80 GB of VRAM, while local storage is absent. Performance benchmarks reveal a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is weak, ranking in the bottom 25%, whereas GPU-driven large language model inference reaches top-tier levels in the top 10% for both prompt processing and text generation. Consequently, the qualitative cost efficiency of this instance is highly dependent on GPU utilization. The server is optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads such as machine learning inference, deep learning, and data science, rather than general-purpose or CPU-bound applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80GB VRAM, optimized for top-tier LLM inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g features 24 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized architecture pairing dual NVIDIA A100 GPUs with dedicated Intel Xeon processors for high-throughput parallel computing and machine learning.
bullet_points: Dual NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80 GB total VRAM
24 vCPUs and 12 physical Intel Xeon cores with 170.0 GB RAM
Top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference
Weak single-core CPU performance representing a key architectural tradeoff
No local storage included in the default configuration
Best suited for machine learning, deep learning, and GPU-accelerated data science
